seoTitle: How to Put Two 5x7 Images on One Page in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to fit two 5x7 images onto a single page in Canva to save time and paper on invitations, flyers, and photo layouts.
intro: Fitting two 5x7 images onto one page can save time and printing resources for invitations, flyers, or photo collages. This tutorial covers setting up your project, placing the images, and exporting them ready for print.

steps.heading: Set up your Canva project
steps.body: Start a new project sized appropriately to fit two 5x7 images on a single page.

steps.heading: Upload and place your images
steps.body: Add your images to the page and position them side by side.

steps.heading: Adjust sizes and spacing
steps.body: Fine-tune spacing and alignment so both images fit neatly and look polished.

steps.heading: Finalize your design
steps.body: Review the layout before moving to export.

steps.heading: Export your project
steps.body: Save the finished design in a format ready for printing.

faq.question: Why would I put two 5x7 images on one page?
faq.answer: This technique saves both time and printing resources, which is useful for invitations, flyers, or photo collages.

faq.question: Is this useful for business marketing materials?
faq.answer: Yes, businesses can use this layout for event announcements, ads, or marketing materials that need multiple visuals.

faq.question: Do I need prior design experience?
faq.answer: No prior experience is required, and even beginners can quickly grasp how to create this layout.

faq.question: Does Canva assist with alignment automatically?
faq.answer: Canva's AI functionalities can help automate certain design elements, making alignment and spacing smoother.
